homepage.heroSubline: Preserving the City of Brotherly Love, one brick at a time. Serving historic neighborhoods with precision craftsmanship since 2012.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Onyx Masonry grew from a single truck operation to Philadelphia's most requested masonry team. We've repointed Rittenhouse Square brownstones and rebuilt Fishtown garden walls through blizzards and heat waves alike.
Our approach blends traditional techniques with modern materials. Whether matching 19th century brickwork or installing weather-resistant stoops, we treat every project like it's on our own block.
From sponsoring neighborhood cleanups to training local apprentices, we're invested in Philadelphia's future as much as its historic fabric.

services.name: Historic Brick Repointing
services.shortDescription: Breathing new life into Philadelphia's aging brickwork with period-accurate techniques.
services.description:
Philadelphia's freeze-thaw cycles wreak havoc on century-old mortar joints. Our craftsmen carefully remove degraded mortar without damaging original bricks, then repoint with custom lime-based mixes that match historic compositions. For Rittenhouse Square townhomes and Society Hill row houses alike, we replicate traditional tuckpointing styles while improving weather resistance.

Special attention goes into preventing efflorescence - that white salt buildup common on Philly's brick facades. We analyze your wall's exposure to road salt from winter storms and adjust our mixtures accordingly. All work includes a 10-year guarantee against spalling and moisture penetration.
process: Step 1: Hand-remove deteriorated mortar to 2x joint depth
Step 2: Match original mortar color/texture using local aggregate
Step 3: Install weather-resistant backer rod in deep joints
Step 4: Apply new mortar with hawk and trowel for historic accuracy
benefits: Prevents structural damage from water infiltration

faq.question: How often should Philadelphia row houses be repointed?
faq.answer: Most Center City properties need repointing every 30-50 years. We inspect for telltale signs like crumbling mortar or loose bricks during free assessments.

faq.question: Will repointing disrupt my neighbors in attached homes?
faq.answer: We use dust containment systems and coordinate with adjacent property owners - critical for tight Queen Village lots.

services.name: Stoop Restoration
services.shortDescription: Rebuilding Philadelphia's iconic entry steps to withstand decades more use.
services.description:
The classic Philly stoop faces unique challenges - from winter salt damage to settling in our clay-heavy soil. We don't just patch cracks; we completely reconstruct problematic steps using steel-reinforced concrete mixed for our freeze-thaw climate. For historic districts like Old City, we replicate original profiles and decorative elements.

Our process begins with analyzing why your stoop failed. In South Philly, we often find inadequate original footings. In Manayunk, it's typically water infiltration behind steps. Solutions range from helical piers for stabilization to waterproof membranes beneath new treads. We also install slip-resistant finishes - crucial for those icy February mornings.
process: Step 1: Demolish damaged sections with minimal vibration
Step 2: Excavate and reinforce foundation as needed
Step 3: Pour custom forms matching original dimensions
Step 4: Apply durable finish coat with traction aggregate
benefits: Improves accessibility with proper rise/run ratios

faq.question: Why do Philadelphia stoops fail so often?
faq.answer: Many were built directly on unstable fill dirt. We install proper gravel bases and drainage to prevent recurring issues.

faq.question: Can you match my original decorative elements?
faq.answer: Our workshop recreates everything from Colonial-era scalloping to Victorian-era bullnoses using historic molds.

services.name: Chimney Restoration
services.shortDescription: Securing Philadelphia's chimney stacks against harsh winters and high winds.
services.description:
Nor'easters topple dozens of Philadelphia chimneys each year. Our complete rebuilds use reinforced flue liners and aircraft cable bracing to prevent collapses. For brick chimneys in Chestnut Hill's stone homes, we carefully dismantle and rebuild section by section. In Fishtown's industrial conversions, we install stainless steel caps to prevent downdrafts.

We specialize in diagnosing subtle chimney leans - often the first sign of foundation issues in Philly's older homes. Thermal imaging helps identify heat-damaged mortar before it becomes hazardous. All work complies with Philadelphia's strict masonry code for attached dwellings, including firestop installation between row houses.
process: Step 1: Remove compromised bricks/coping stones
Step 2: Install seismic reinforcement system
Step 3: Rebuild with high-temp refractory mortar
Step 4: Seal against water penetration

faq.question: How urgent is chimney repair in Philadelphia?
faq.answer: Any visible lean or loose bricks requires immediate attention - winter winds can topple unstable stacks onto rowhouse roofs.

faq.question: Do you work on industrial flues in converted factories?
faq.answer: Yes, we've restored dozens of Northern Liberties loft chimneys using specialized high-capacity liners.

services.name: Stone Foundation Stabilization
services.shortDescription: Arresting settlement in Philadelphia's vintage stone basements.
services.description:
Many Philadelphia homes rest on 18th-19th century stone foundations prone to shifting in our region's clay soil. Unlike quick-fix contractors, we address root causes through proper drainage and helical pier installation. For Society Hill's colonial homes, we meticulously repack loose rubble stone with hydraulic lime grout - strong enough to stabilize, flexible enough to accommodate movement.

Our process begins with laser leveling to identify problem areas. In Queen Village, we often find deteriorated mortar washed out by old downspout drainage. In Germantown, it's typically tree root intrusion. Solutions range from French drain installation to carbon fiber wall reinforcement, always preserving original character.
process: Step 1: Excavate problem areas with archaeological care
Step 2: Install perimeter drainage where needed
Step 3: Inject stabilizing grout or install piers
Step 4: Re-point with flexible historic mortar

faq.question: How do I know if my stone foundation needs repair?
faq.answer: Look for stair-step cracks in above-ground brickwork or doors/windows that no longer close properly - common in Fairmount's older homes.

faq.question: Will this disrupt my finished basement?
faq.Answer: We use minimally invasive techniques - most projects don't require removing paneling or flooring.

services.name: Garden Wall Construction
services.shortDescription: Building private outdoor spaces worthy of Philadelphia's hidden gardens.
services.description:
From Spruce Hill's community gardens to Rittenhouse Square's private oases, we build walls that complement Philadelphia's architectural diversity. Our load-bearing designs incorporate proper frost footings - critical in our freeze-thaw climate. For historic districts, we source reclaimed brick to match surrounding buildings. Modern installations feature weather-resistant segmental blocks with classic Philly styling.

We pay special attention to drainage behind walls, a common failure point in our heavy rains. All designs include weep holes and gravel backfill to prevent hydrostatic pressure buildup. For sloped lots in East Falls, we engineer retaining walls with proper terracing and reinforcement.
process: Step 1: Excavate to frost line (36" in Philadelphia)
Step 2: Install compacted stone base
Step 3: Lay courses with proper bonding patterns
Step 4: Cap with durable coping stones

faq.question: What permits are needed for Philly garden walls?
faq.answer: Walls over 4' require permits - we handle all paperwork including zoning reviews in protected districts.

faq.question: Can you match my home's historic brick?
faq.answer: Our network of local suppliers carries 200+ historic brick types common in Philadelphia architecture.

reviews.name: Marianne K.
reviews.text: Onyx saved our 1920s Graduate Hospital row house after a disastrous experience with another contractor. They matched the original mortar color perfectly and reinforced our parapet wall to prevent future leaks. Two winters later - zero new cracks despite record snowfall.

reviews.name: Devon R.
reviews.text: Our Brewerytown stoop was literally crumbling beneath us. Onyx not only rebuilt it with proper footings, but added subtle traction strips that have prevented slips during icy winters. They even matched the original 1930s bullnose detail our neighbors admired.

reviews.name: Lisa T.
reviews.text: When Hurricane Ida damaged our Mt. Airy chimney, Onyx had it braced within 24 hours. Their meticulous reconstruction included installing a new flue liner and custom copper flashing that handles our heavy rains beautifully. The mason even pointed out some roof issues we hadn't noticed.

reviews.name: Carlos M.
reviews.text: Our Old City tavern's 18th century foundation was shifting alarmingly. Onyx's helical pier system stabilized everything without disturbing the original stonework. They uncovered and repaired old drainage tunnels that were causing the problem - now our basement stays dry even during Nor'easters.

reviews.name: Nia W.
reviews.text: Onyx transformed our Passyunk Square backyard with a beautiful brick wall that matches our home's 1910 facade. They engineered proper drainage that's handled record rainfall without a single leak. The mason even salvaged some old bricks from our property to incorporate into the design.

reviews.name: Thomas B.
reviews.text: When a drunk driver hit our Fairmount garden wall at 2AM, Onyx had a crew out by sunrise to secure the structure. Their temporary shoring held until we could schedule a full rebuild that perfectly matched the original. Crisis handled with zero stress on our part.

service-areas.title: Philadelphia Masonry Service Areas | Onyx Masonry
service-areas.description: Serving Philadelphia and surrounding communities with complete masonry restoration and repair services.

serviceAreas.content:
Onyx Masonry proudly serves Philadelphia, the heart of Pennsylvania and a city rich in history and architectural beauty. Our expert masons specialize in restoring historic brickwork, constructing durable stone walls, and creating custom masonry features that enhance both residential and commercial properties. Whether you need repairs for a classic Philadelphia rowhome or a new installation for a modern building, we deliver top-quality craftsmanship tailored to the city's unique character.

Philadelphia's diverse neighborhoods each have their own architectural styles, from the cobblestone streets of Old City to the brownstones of Rittenhouse Square. Our team understands these local nuances and uses materials and techniques that preserve the integrity of each area. We take pride in contributing to the preservation and enhancement of Philadelphia's iconic masonry structures.
serviceAreas.localContext: Philadelphia's historic architecture demands skilled masons who understand preservation techniques.

serviceAreas.content:
Onyx Masonry brings exceptional masonry services to Conshohocken, a vibrant borough along the Schuylkill River. We specialize in creating beautiful stone patios, retaining walls, and brick facades that complement the area's mix of historic and contemporary homes. Our work in Conshohocken often involves blending traditional masonry techniques with modern designs to meet the needs of this growing community.

Many Conshohocken properties feature stunning river views, and our outdoor masonry work is designed to enhance these natural assets. From fire pits to elegant walkways, we create functional and attractive hardscape elements that withstand the region's seasonal changes while adding value to your property.
serviceAreas.localContext: Conshohocken's riverside location makes outdoor masonry features particularly valuable.

serviceAreas.content:

Our chimney repair and repointing services are particularly sought after in Ardmore's older neighborhoods.

For newer constructions, we create custom masonry elements that match Ardmore's sophisticated aesthetic. Whether it's a grand stone entrance, a decorative brick wall, or a cozy outdoor fireplace, our designs reflect the elegance and quality that Ardmore residents expect.
serviceAreas.localContext: Ardmore's Main Line heritage requires masonry that meets high aesthetic standards.
